exemplar.title: M25 #3: How can we reconcile the relentless drive to pursue knowledge with the finite resources we have available? Discuss with reference to the natural sciences and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #3: How can we reconcile the relentless drive to pursue knowledge with the finite resources we have available? Discuss with reference to the natural sciences and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Clear and sustained focus on the prescribed title, with explicit definitions of key terms.
Effective linkage to two areas of knowledge (natural sciences and human sciences) with a coherent structure.
Use of multiple real-world examples to illustrate claims, demonstrating a good range of factual support.
Awareness of alternative perspectives, notably in the debate over resource allocation.
weaknesses: Discussion remains largely descriptive, with limited depth of analysis linking examples to the central knowledge question.
Counter-arguments are brief and thinly evaluated, lacking exploration of their implications.
Coherence is uneven in places; sections sometimes read as loosely connected anecdotes rather than integrated elements of a sustained argument.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The strand assessment placed the essay in the 5â6 band for âUnderstanding Knowledge Questions,â noting clear focus, relevant examples and some awareness of alternative views, but limited depth of analysis and uneven coherence. The official examinerâs total mark of 6 must be distributed to this criterion, elevating it to the top of the 5â6 band to reflect overall performance. A score of 6/10 appropriately acknowledges the essayâs strengths in focus and illustration while accounting for its descriptive nature and minimal evaluative rigour.

annotations.comment: The Ishango bone example is descriptive but lacks deeper analysis of how finite resource constraints influenced early mathematical reasoning; link more explicitly to the knowledge question.
The opening thesis introduces strategic prioritisation, but it would benefit from linking this directly to a TOK framework (e.g., implications for methods of inquiry).
While definitions are clear, the student could enhance depth by relating them to different types of knowledge (e.g., personal vs. shared) within TOK.
The counter-argument considering redirection of resources to terrestrial issues demonstrates awareness of alternative perspectives in resource prioritisation.
The conclusion reiterates the inevitability of human curiosity and the necessity of prioritisation, closing the essay with a coherent restatement of the thesis.
The shift to space exploration lacks a clear link to the prior resource definition; a stronger transition could emphasise how these resources are prioritized across AOKs.
The political and technological motivations behind Sputnik are well explained, illustrating the interplay of necessity and curiosity in the natural sciences.
The COVID-19 behavioural research example is relevant but needs clearer analysis of how resource allocation influenced research focus and outcomes.
The final remark on vaccine mandates would be stronger if linked back to finite resource considerations, rather than presented as an isolated point.
Including the word count here is unnecessary; the essay should focus on content rather than administrative details.
The phrase âconsciously disregarding othersâ introduces an ethical dimension; explore its implications for impartiality and bias in knowledge selection.
The clear delineation of tangible vs. intangible resources deepens the analysis of resource constraints across different modes of inquiry.
The social norms vaccination example would benefit from contrasting it with virology research to illustrate trade-offs in the human sciences.
The introduction maintains a sustained focus on the prescribed title and explicitly outlines the areas of knowledge to be explored, providing a clear roadmap for the essay.
Definitions of key terms (âknowledge,â âreconciliationâ) are clearly stated, establishing a solid foundation for the discussion.
This neurobiological account, while informative, overshadows explicit TOK analysis of knowledge claims; consider discussing how dopamine circuits affect reliability of knowledge.
The fire discovery example remains largely descriptive; it needs analysis of how resource availability shaped experimentation and validation in the natural sciences.
The discussion of Earthâs finite resources offers interesting scientific background but lacks explicit connection to how resource scarcity constrains knowledge frameworks.
Expanding âfinite resourcesâ to include intangible factors (attention spans, institutional focus) shows nuanced understanding of resource allocation in knowledge pursuits.
The description of epistemic curiosity is detailed and effectively introduces a human science perspective, showcasing understanding of psychological drivers of knowledge.
The transition to necessity in human sciences (John Snow) is cut short; ensure the full example demonstrates the trade-off between epistemic aims and resource constraints.
The John Snow cholera case is a strong illustration of necessity-driven inquiry, clearly linking finite medical resources to knowledge production in epidemiology.
Claims about resource extraction from other planets require critical evaluation of feasibility and ethical implications, not only speculative benefits.
When presenting the fire discovery, tie it explicitly to specific ways of knowing (e.g., experimentation, sense perception) to strengthen the TOK link.
The account of Earthâs formation is scientifically accurate but diverges from focus; consider whether this level of detail advances your argument.

1â5 or is not a

The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
